mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when INDPRO increases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.5% transmits restrictive monetary policy into equity markets by raising discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%, positive 60-day SPX returns occur more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in nonfarm payrolls alongside decreases in the unemployment rate signal a tightening labor market that boosts aggregate wage income and consumption, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods where PAYEMS rises and UNRATE falls jointly fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during late-cycle expansions when consumer spending contributes the marginal share of GDP growth.
failure_condition: Positive surprises in RSAFS during late-cycle expansions coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production rising faster than retail sales indicates supply is catching up to demand, which relieves input cost pressures and supports profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When INDPRO growth exceeds RSAFS growth, positive 60-day SPX returns occur in fewer than 55% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative increase in the unemployment rate greater than 0.3 percentage points over two consecutive months signals labor market deterioration that precedes downward earnings revisions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Falling consumer prices combined with stable or rising payroll employment indicate a disinflationary expansion where real discount rates decline without recession risk, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint observations of month-over-month falling CPIAUCSL and rising PAYEMS fail to coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal durable demand for construction materials, labor, and credit, which translates into higher cyclical corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising HOUST fails to coincide with positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations.
